ownedCards.name: Crocodile
ownedCards.title: 2022 ST03 The Seven Warlords of the Sea #001 Crocodile Super Pre-Release
ownedCards.thesis: Early Seven Warlords leader with Super Pre-Release framing. This matters more as a theme/era piece than as a raw power play. It becomes more relevant around OP-14 Warlords attention.
ownedCards.risk: PSA 9 caps upside versus PSA 10; Crocodile demand is strong but below Luffy/Zoro/Nami/Roger.
ownedCards.trigger: Sell only into a Warlords/Crocodile hype premium or if you can upgrade to PSA 10 without overpaying.

ownedCards.name: Monkey.D.Luffy
ownedCards.title: 2025 Japanese Promo 3rd Anniversary Treasure SR Luffy #ST10-006
ownedCards.thesis: Luffy plus anniversary/promo context is the strongest fit in the current collection. Main-character demand gives this a better floor than many cheap modern slabs.
ownedCards.risk: Japanese modern promos can be reprinted or repriced quickly; CGC 10 does not always command PSA 10 liquidity.
ownedCards.trigger: Hold unless a PSA 10 comp premium appears large enough to justify selling or cross-grading risk.

ownedCards.name: Zoro-Juurou
ownedCards.title: 2024 Japanese SD Purple Luffy SR Zoro-Juurou #ST18-004
ownedCards.thesis: Personal brand fit for Zoro, strong character, Pristine label, and purple Straw Hat deck relevance. This is a collection identity card.
ownedCards.risk: Starter deck cards are more supply-sensitive than event promos or top chase rarities.
ownedCards.trigger: Hold through shipment; consider selling only if Courtyard/local buyers pay a strong pristine-label premium.

ownedCards.name: Franky
ownedCards.title: 2023 Premium Collection Film Red Alt Art Franky #OP01-021
ownedCards.thesis: Nice Film Red tie-in and high grade. Good visual card, but Franky is not a top liquidity character.
ownedCards.risk: Lower character liquidity; easy to hold too long if no specific buyer appears.
ownedCards.trigger: List or trade if it can fund Luffy/Zoro/Nami/Roger/Ace/Sabo upgrades.

ownedCards.name: Nami
ownedCards.title: 2023 Tournament Pack Vol. 3 Nami #ST01-007
ownedCards.thesis: Nami is one of the most liquid non-Luffy One Piece characters and this is the visible-value anchor of the book. The portfolio is currently concentrated around this position.
ownedCards.risk: CGC 9 is not a premium grade; if Collectr value is stale or thin, do not treat the $400 as instantly realizable.
ownedCards.trigger: Verify sold comps after arrival. Sell only if a buyer pays close to high comps, or upgrade into PSA 10/CGC 10 version.

ownedCards.name: Monkey.D.Luffy
ownedCards.title: 2024 ST18 Purple Luffy #041 Monkey D. Luffy
ownedCards.thesis: Low-dollar main-character slab. Not a trophy, but Luffy liquidity is useful when building a bigger Courtyard shipment.
ownedCards.risk: PSA 9 modern starter/promo cards can be common; do not average up heavily.
ownedCards.trigger: Keep if shipping anyway; sell first if it blocks capital for PSA 10 Sabo/Roger/Ace.

ownedCards.name: Carrot
ownedCards.title: 2024 OP08 Two Legends #023 Carrot
ownedCards.thesis: PSA 10 is good, and Carrot has fans, but she is not core to the Ghost x Zoro Luffy/Warlords/Roger/Ace/Sabo thesis.
ownedCards.risk: May sit longer than main-character slabs. Good grade does not equal strong demand.
ownedCards.trigger: Use as first quick sale/trade if you need room or funds before shipping.

ownedCards.name: The Dark Magicians
ownedCards.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Dark Magicians
ownedCards.thesis: Not a bad card, but it dilutes a One Piece-focused thesis. Use it as trade fuel.
ownedCards.risk: Keeping mixed-IP inventory makes the strategy harder to track.
ownedCards.trigger: Convert to One Piece sealed, Luffy/Zoro/Nami/Roger/Ace/Sabo slabs, or cash reserve.

offerDecisions.name: Sabo
offerDecisions.rationale: Best risk/reward in the visible offer book. OP-13 anniversary context, popular character, PSA 10, tiny bid size.
offerDecisions.cap: Keep up to roughly the low $30s unless live comps say otherwise.

offerDecisions.name: Monkey.D.Luffy B&W Alt
offerDecisions.rationale: Egghead Luffy and ST-29 are current-cycle demand. PSA 9 is acceptable at the current bid if you keep discipline.
offerDecisions.cap: Do not chase far above $30-$35 without verified comps.

offerDecisions.name: Gol.D.Roger
offerDecisions.rationale: Roger is a franchise apex character, and the card text/identity is iconic. PSA 9 is fine at low cost.

offerDecisions.name: Portgas D. Ace
offerDecisions.rationale: Ace has emotional collector demand, pairs with Luffy/Sabo thesis, and the bid is small.
offerDecisions.cap: Hold if won; sell only into fast premium.

offerDecisions.name: Monkey D. Luffy
offerDecisions.rationale: Early starter Luffy is on-thesis, but not rare enough to become a big position.
offerDecisions.cap: Keep if shipment quality stays high; do not duplicate.

offerDecisions.name: Edward Newgate
offerDecisions.rationale: Whitebeard is iconic and pristine label helps. CGC liquidity is lower than PSA.
offerDecisions.cap: Keep at current bid; do not overextend.

offerDecisions.name: Monkey D. Luffy PRB01
offerDecisions.rationale: Luffy is liquid, but PRB reprint context reduces scarcity. Fine only because the bid is low.
offerDecisions.cap: Flip candidate if won and comps are thin.

offerDecisions.name: Marshall D. Teach
offerDecisions.rationale: Blackbeard is a real villain thesis, and $8 is low. But it is still a CGC 10 non-trophy.
offerDecisions.cap: Keep only if you want a villain slot.

offerDecisions.name: Vinsmoke Reiju
offerDecisions.rationale: Popular character and low bid, but not aligned with the current crew thesis.
offerDecisions.cap: Cancel if you want a cleaner shipment.

offerDecisions.name: Marco
offerDecisions.rationale: Pristine label helps, but Marco is not a first-wave capital target for this portfolio.
offerDecisions.cap: Keep only as a cheap flip.

offerDecisions.name: Sabo
offerDecisions.rationale: Duplicate exposure. The PSA 10 bid is only $1 higher, so the PSA 9 is inefficient unless both are deliberate ladder bids.
offerDecisions.cap: Cancel before capital locks if you can.

offerDecisions.name: Gol.D.Roger
offerDecisions.rationale: Low grade and duplicate subject. Cheap does not mean investable.
offerDecisions.cap: Avoid low-grade modern slabs unless they are iconic scarce promos.

offerDecisions.name: Shiki
offerDecisions.rationale: Old pre-Bandai curiosity, low grade, weak fit with the modern One Piece TCG thesis.
offerDecisions.cap: Cancel and keep the slot for Luffy/Roger/Ace/Sabo/Nami/Zoro.

sources.name: PSA infrastructure demand
sources.note: PSA says it graded 2M cards in 2020 and more than 19M in 2025; Jan-Apr 2026 output was up 39% year over year.

sources.name: PSA x Bandai promo signal
sources.note: PSA says the first PSA/Bandai Straw Hat promo became the most-graded English One Piece card of all time.

sources.name: PSA One Piece basics
sources.note: Useful for set-code, rarity, promo, franchise, and modern Bandai TCG context.

sources.name: Bandai card business growth
sources.note: Bandai discusses global card-game growth, regional strategy, supply capacity, localization, TCG+ data, and fan demand.

sources.name: OP-14 The Azure Sea's Seven
sources.note: Official page: Seven Warlords theme, January 16, 2026 English release, MSRP $4.99, 163+2 card types.

sources.name: ST-29 Egghead
sources.note: Official page: Egghead starter, January 16, 2026 release, $16.99 MSRP, Luffy leader, Rush: Character and Unblockable.

sources.name: TCG Portal release tracker
sources.note: Non-official release tracker: ST31-ST36 July 11, 2026; OP-17 listed for August 22, 2026 in Japan. Treat as schedule intelligence until Bandai English page appears.

sources.name: TCGplayer sellers
sources.note: Seller channel with global reach, listing/pricing/fulfillment tools, and marketplace trust layer.

sources.name: Cardmarket One Piece
sources.note: European market surface for One Piece comps, trends, best sellers, singles, sealed, and cross-market arbitrage checks.

sources.name: eBay Vault fees
sources.note: Vault/fee source to check before moving higher-value graded slabs into eBay fulfillment.

sources.name: Collect-a-Con Cape Town
sources.note: Local event surface for in-person buying, selling, trade, auction, and vendor networking.

sources.name: Card Cache Cape Town
sources.note: Cape Town store signal for TCG singles, sealed, and local liquidity checks.

sources.name: TCGXchange South Africa
sources.note: South African online TCG store surface for stock, preorder, and local pricing comparison.

sources.name: Wizards Books and Games
sources.note: Local hobby-store surface to verify events, stock, and buy/sell opportunities.

sources.name: Official One Piece Card List
sources.note: Official card art and gameplay text used for private analytical slab renders.

caveats: Educational scenario analysis only, not financial advice.
No raw account-sensitive details are included.
Slab visuals are analytical renders using official card art unless a platform image is explicitly labeled.
OP-17 August 2026 is treated as schedule intelligence from non-official/retailer sources until official Bandai English publication is verified.
